task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz application is open, and the title bar or scene browser indicates that 'dwanko_run.tnz' is the active loaded scene.

task_completion.description: Access the timing chart to determine the required rhythm.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., gedit) or terminal window is visible displaying the contents of '/home/ga/Desktop/timing_chart.txt'.

task_completion.description: Modify the Xsheet exposures to match the timing chart.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Xsheet (timeline) panel in OpenToonz shows modified cell exposures. Specifically, look for Column 5 (the character animation) having cells stretched or arranged to match the hold pattern: 4 frames, 2 frames, 2 frames, 2 frames, 10 frames, and 4 frames.

task_completion.description: Configure the render settings for a 24-frame PNG sequence to the correct output directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Output Settings' dialog in OpenToonz is open. The 'Save In' path is set to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/retimed/', the file format is set to 'png', and the frame range (From/To) is set to 1 to 24.

task_completion.description: Render the modified animation as a PNG sequence.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A rendering progress bar in OpenToonz is visible, OR a file manager/terminal window shows the newly generated PNG files (e.g., 0001.png to 0024.png) in the '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/retimed/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used OpenToonz to render the sequence rather than using terminal commands to fake the output.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the OpenToonz 'Render' menu or Output Settings, rather than ImageMagick/ffmpeg commands in the terminal generating the PNGs.

integrity.description: The agent actually modified the Xsheet in OpenToonz.
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz Xsheet must visibly reflect the new timing holds (e.g., blocks of 4, 2, 2, 2, 10, 4 frames) rather than the agent just copying and renaming files in the file explorer.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual character animation column.
integrity.visual_evidence: Modifications should be visible in Column 5 of the Xsheet (where the character is), not just Column 1 (which contains the sky background).

privileged_info_for_vlm: The timing chart specifies 24 frames with holds at frames 1-4, 5-6, 7-8, 9-10, 11-20, and 21-24. The character animation is located in Column 5 of the Xsheet, not Column 1 (which is the sky). Drawings 7 and 9 do not physically exist in the original file. Therefore, a visual change at frame 11 (transitioning from drawing 7 to 9) is impossible and will result in blank frames; do not penalize the agent if frames 11-20 appear blank or lack a visual change, as long as the Xsheet exposure holds are set correctly. The final output must be a 24-frame PNG sequence saved to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/retimed/'.
